text.insert: This doc measures our canvas engine's shape vocabulary against FigJam's default shape picker — the same picker used as ground truth throughout this docs tree (see the
"FigJam visual parity is ground truth" decision). The picker has three tiers, and they map onto our schema very differently:
values — Basic (14) + Flowchart (16) = 30 enum entries in Figma's
, each a first-class shape a user can place directly. This is the tier our 31
, not shapes at all. Compared against our
values (they don't appear anywhere in the plugin typings' shape enum). Treated here as a separate icon tier, compared against our
Every row below gets one of three verdicts, judged strictly against actual rendering code (in
), not against type names that merely sound similar:
— geometry and role match; minor styling deltas (fixed radius, palette) are noted but don't demote the verdict.
— we render something for this slot, but its silhouette, geometry, or behavior diverges enough that it isn't a faithful stand-in.
for the four-facet contract (geometry, style, text slot, connection anchors) every shape satisfies, and
for exhaustive per-type rendering detail. This doc is the gap analysis that sits between them.
Reconciling the shared brief's 59-item picker transcription against the plugin typings' verbatim
enum: Basic's 14 + Flowchart's 16 map one-to-one onto all 30
values with none left over. The 3 Connectors are
line-type presets, not shapes. The 26 Advanced items are components — they don't appear in the
union at all, and are handled in their own subsection and strategy note below.

FigJam name               shapeType          verdict  our mapping                     notes
------------------------  -----------------  -------  ------------------------------  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Square (rounded corners)  SQUARE             HAVE     rounded-rect (default)          our fixed 8px radius vs Figma's tighter square corner; close enough
Circle                    ELLIPSE            HAVE     ellipse                         true ellipse inscribed in the bounding box, true-outline anchors (W5)
Diamond                   DIAMOND            HAVE     decision (style.shape:diamond)  CSS clip-path 4-point diamond, true outline anchors
Triangle (up)             TRIANGLE_UP        HAVE     triangle (direction:up)         isosceles triangle polygon, true-outline anchors (W5)
Triangle (down)           TRIANGLE_DOWN      HAVE     triangle (direction:down)       same primitive as TRIANGLE_UP, flipped via the direction field (W5)
Rounded rectangle (wide)  ROUNDED_RECTANGLE  HAVE     rectangle/process (default)     same rounded-rect as square, just wider aspect
Pentagon                  PENTAGON           HAVE     pentagon                        5-point regular polygon, true-outline anchors (W5)
Octagon                   OCTAGON            HAVE     octagon                         8-point regular polygon, true-outline anchors (W5)
Plus/cross                PLUS               HAVE     plus                            12-point cross polygon, true-outline anchors; renders no visible label text (a11y-only) (W5)
Block arrow (left)        ARROW_LEFT         HAVE     arrow-shape (direction:left)    bodyHeightRatio raised 0.53->0.60 in W5 so the body reads blockier; approximate pending pixel sample
Block arrow (right)       ARROW_RIGHT        HAVE     arrow-shape (direction:right)   same bodyHeightRatio 0.60 update as ARROW_LEFT; approximate pending pixel sample
Fat chevron arrow         CHEVRON            HAVE     chevron                         landed as its own object type distinct from arrow-shape, not a weight variant (W5)
Star                      STAR               HAVE     star                            5-point star polygon, true-outline anchors; inner-radius ratio flagged approximate pending pixel-reference check (W5)
Speech bubble             SPEECH_BUBBLE      HAVE     icon (glyph: chat)              Chat catalog entry stamps type icon with glyph chat after D21
0.60 — raised from 0.53 in W5 — in objects/geometry.ts's

) — FigJam's block arrow is a blockier, full-height arrow body; the W5 bump closes most of the former thin-sliver silhouette gap (still flagged approximate pending a live pixel sample). Same geometry as the right-pointing variant.
Block arrow (right) / ARROW_RIGHT: Same bodyHeightRatio 0.60 geometry (and the same W5 blockier-body update) as the left-pointing variant — both are the same
Speech bubble is HAVE via the catalog's
mapping; there is no separate speech-bubble object type after D21.

FigJam name                         shapeType            verdict  our mapping                      notes
----------------------------------  -------------------  -------  -------------------------------  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Parallelogram (skew right)          PARALLELOGRAM_RIGHT  HAVE     parallelogram (direction:right)  skewPx a fixed fraction of width; flagged approximate pending pixel-reference check (W5)
Parallelogram (skew left)           PARALLELOGRAM_LEFT   HAVE     parallelogram (direction:left)   same skew-ratio approximation as PARALLELOGRAM_RIGHT (W5)
Cylinder vertical (database/drum)   ENG_DATABASE         HAVE     database                         true geometric cylinder: closed barrel path + ellipse lid, matches FigJam's silhouette closely
Cylinder horizontal                 ENG_QUEUE            HAVE     cylinder-horizontal              dedicated horizontal-orientation cylinder primitive, bbox-fallback outline (W5)
Page with folded corner             ENG_FILE             HAVE     page-corner                      the old dog-ear/folded-corner notch moved verbatim from document to this new dedicated type (W5)
Folder (tabbed)                     ENG_FOLDER           HAVE     folder                           rectangle with a tab notch on the top edge, bbox-fallback outline (W5)
Document (wavy bottom edge)         DOCUMENT_SINGLE      HAVE     document                         document now renders a genuine wavy/sinusoidal bottom edge via documentWavyPath; the dog-ear moved to page-corner (W5)
Multiple documents (stacked)        DOCUMENT_MULTIPLE    HAVE     document-stack                   offset duplicate wavy-document outlines behind the primary, bbox-fallback outline (W5)
Predefined process                  PREDEFINED_PROCESS   HAVE     predefined-process               rounded-rect + two inset vertical bars near left/right edges, matches the FigJam symbol
Off-page connector (pentagon down)  SHIELD               HAVE     off-page-connector               downward-pointing pentagon, true-outline anchors (W5)
Trapezoid (manual operation)        TRAPEZOID            HAVE     trapezoid                        4-point trapezoid polygon, true-outline anchors (W5)
Manual input (slanted top edge)     MANUAL_INPUT         HAVE     manual-input                     rectangle with a slanted top edge, true-outline anchors (W5)
Hexagon (preparation)               HEXAGON              HAVE     hexagon                          6-point regular hexagon, flat-top orientation, true-outline anchors (W5)
Internal storage                    INTERNAL_STORAGE     HAVE     internal-storage                 rect + top row + left column rule lines; outline is a plain rect (interior rules don't perturb it) (W5)
Or-junction (circle with +)         OR                   HAVE     or-junction                      ellipse-silhouette with an inscribed '+'; renders no visible label text (a11y-only) (W5)
Summing junction (circle with X)    SUMMING_JUNCTION     HAVE     summing-junction                 ellipse-silhouette with an inscribed 'x'; renders no visible label text (a11y-only) (W5)
Page with folded corner / ENG_FILE: Our
type owns the straight-edged dog-ear clip-path, matching FigJam's ENG_FILE concept;
is the separate wavy-bottom DOCUMENT_SINGLE shape via
type renders a wavy-bottom silhouette via
, matching FigJam's DOCUMENT_SINGLE concept; the straight-edged dog-ear clip-path now belongs to the separate
Off-page connector / SHIELD: Figma's own naming is worth flagging: the Flowchart picker's "off-page connector" (pointing-down pentagon) shows up in the typings as the

shapeType — an odd tooltip/enum-name pairing worth double-checking against a live FigJam instance before implementing, since it doesn't obviously correspond to a shield glyph the way the Advanced tier's own "Shield" icon does.

FigJam name               line type            verdict              our mapping                            notes
------------------------  -------------------  -------------------  -------------------------------------  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Straight line (no arrow)  line, no arrowheads  HAVE                 connection style:solid, arrow:none     orthogonal router snaps aligned endpoints to an exact straight run; zero arrowhead markers
Straight arrow            line, one arrowhead  HAVE-BUT-DEVIATES    connection style:solid, arrow:forward  routing and the single filled triangle arrowhead are solid, but Figma exposes 12 ConnectorStrokeCap variants and independent start/end caps; we expose only none/forward/back/both using that one marker
Elbow arrow               ELBOWED connector    HAVE-BUT-DEVIATES    routed orthogonal connector            elbow-only routing is now the default router behavior, not a style value; bend pills edit persisted waypoints, snap-to-straight clears them, reconnect resets bends, and default connector color deliberately stays gray rather than tinting from the source object
Straight arrow: The routing and arrow placement are fully correct — the remaining gaps are cap variety and a few deliberate interaction/style differences. Figma exposes 12
, etc.) per endpoint, while we have exactly one arrowhead marker (a solid filled triangle,

-colored) with no per-connection choice of cap style. Creation now has two surfaces: select mode keeps selection-only off-bounds anchor dots, while the dedicated Connector Mode hover-reveals dots on any object, ignores the zoom gate, supports body-drag with auto-picked source anchors, and stays sticky for chained creation. This closes the FigJam-parity gap for FigJam's connector tool behavior. At rest, connector labels are 16px/700 midpoint chips.

: our database is a true vertical-cylinder flowchart primitive (ENG_DATABASE tier), same over-scaled mismatch pattern: right silhouette family, wrong tier — Advanced wants a small stacked-disc chip icon, not the full shape.

is the Basic/Flowchart-tier wavy DOCUMENT_SINGLE shape via
, and the folded-corner ENG_FILE silhouette is the separate
, not shapeType primitives — placing one drops a small pre-styled icon-in-a-chip onto the board, not a resizable geometric shape. D21 makes that one
object type parameterized by a required
field drawn from the 26-value CanvasIconGlyph enum, so the chat, cpu, database, file, code, and person concepts are glyphs rather than bespoke object types.
Each spec below follows the shape-API's four-facet contract (geometry/outline, text slot, connection anchors, style surface) plus per-type fields and a suggested implementation path — either a new
variant on an existing type, matching how

field rather than two separate left/right types. Trivially-similar shapes (a triangle that just points the other way, a parallelogram skewed the other way) are grouped into one spec with a direction-style field, for the same reason.
A true ellipse inscribed in the object's bounding box — center
trick (border-radius alone can't give true elliptical connection-anchor projection when the object isn't a perfect square).
Default size 160x120 (wide oval, same default sizing convention as other basic shapes). Text slot centered like other shapes. Connection anchors: joined the true-outline tier in W5 (now

in objects/geometry.ts, where the def-declared outlines live since P3) alongside rect/pill/diamond/arrow-shape — an ellipse's outline-intersection is closed-form (parametric ellipse/line intersection), so it's a natural 5th member, not a bbox-fallback shape. Style surface: fill/stroke/strokeStyle like any basic shape, no special fields. Suggested name: new
each get their own type rather than being style-only variants of
An isosceles triangle inscribed in the bounding box.
— the same points vertically mirrored (apex at
with 3 points, the same rendering strategy as the existing

Default size 140x120. Text slot centered in the lower two-thirds (apex area is too narrow for text). Connection anchors: true-outline triangle intersection joins the polygon tier; the 3-point polygon math is simpler than the diamond's 4-point case already implemented. Style surface: fill/stroke/strokeStyle, standard. Per-type field:
's left/right pattern exactly. Suggested name: new
field on the object (not on style, matching how arrow-shape's direction currently lives — check exact placement against schema.ts's
(skew-right): top edge shifted right relative to bottom — points
a fixed fraction of width (e.g. 15-20%, matching FigJam's visual skew angle — needs a pixel-reference check against a live sample, flag as approximate).
Landed in W5 as a fixed ratio (now in objects/geometry.ts since the theme dispersal), with the approximate-pending-pixel-check flag preserved in its doc comment.

Default size 160x100. Text slot centered (parallelogram's middle band is roughly rectangular so text fits reasonably without special-casing). Connection anchors: 4-point polygon, same tier as diamond. Style surface: standard fill/stroke/strokeStyle. Per-type field:
Pentagon: 5-point regular polygon (one point up), computed as
for i in 0..4, radii scaled independently on x/y to fill the bounding box (non-uniform scale, same
). Octagon: 8-point regular polygon, flat-top orientation (matching FigJam's tile), same parametric construction with i in 0..7.

Default size 140x140 (square-ish, matching regular-polygon aspect). Text slot centered. Connection anchors: both are natural additions to the true-outline polygon tier (parametric point lists, same treatment as diamond/arrow-shape). Style surface: standard. Suggested names: two new types,
(not worth grouping into one field — they're a different point count and a shared "regular-polygon with N sides" field would be over-engineering for exactly two consumers).

A 5-point star polygon: 10 alternating outer/inner vertices around the bounding box center, outer radius touching the box edge, inner radius a fixed fraction (~0.4x outer, matching FigJam's visual proportions — flag as approximate pending a pixel-reference check).
Landed in W5 as a fixed ratio (now in objects/geometry.ts since the theme dispersal), with the approximate-pending-pixel-check flag preserved in its doc comment.

Default size 140x140. Text slot centered (small — a star's usable text area is its middle third). Connection anchors: 10-point polygon joins the true-outline tier the same way diamond/triangle do. Style surface: standard. Suggested name: new

A 12-point cross/plus polygon: a horizontal bar and vertical bar of equal thickness crossing at the object's center, thickness a fixed fraction of the smaller bounding-box dimension (~1/3, matching FigJam's proportions).

Default size 120x120 (square — a plus reads oddly at non-square aspects). Text slot: none recommended — a plus's center intersection is too small for a meaningful text slot at typical sizes; treat as a decorative/marker shape like
, not a label-bearing one. Connection anchors: 12-point polygon, true-outline tier. Style surface: standard fill/stroke. Suggested name: new
so the rendered body reads blockier, closing most of the ARROW_LEFT/ARROW_RIGHT gap (flagged approximate pending a live pixel sample). Separately, FigJam's
("fat chevron arrow") landed as its own dedicated
object type rather than a weight variant of arrow-shape — the two silhouettes turned out distinct enough (arrow-shape keeps its 7-point sliver chevron; chevron is its own notch-based polygon via
, proportioned by CHEVRON_GEOMETRY.notchWidthRatio) to justify separate types instead of a shared field.
A manila-folder silhouette: a rectangle with a small tab notch on the top edge (typically top-left, ~35-40% of the width), rendered as a single closed SVG
— the same construction strategy as the existing
wavy SVG path (one path, not a rect + separate tab overlay).
Default size 140x110. Text slot centered in the main body, below the tab. Connection anchors: bbox fallback is acceptable here (matching how

/etc. already fall back to bbox despite having non-rectangular SVG silhouettes) — the tab notch is a small enough perturbation that a true-outline projection isn't worth the complexity. Style surface: standard fill/stroke. Suggested name: new
A rectangle with a sinusoidal/wave-curved bottom edge — 2-3 wave crests via SVG cubic Bézier
commands, top and side edges straight, rendered as a single closed
. This is the flowchart symbol our
; the folded-corner ENG_FILE silhouette moved to
Default size 150x110. Text slot centered in the upper two-thirds (above the wave). Connection anchors: bbox fallback for the wavy silhouette;
remain bbox-fallback precedents. Style surface: standard. Current status:
is now the wavy-bottom DOCUMENT_SINGLE shape, and the folded-corner ENG_FILE shape is the separate

Two or three copies of the (wavy-bottom, per Spec 9) document silhouette, offset diagonally by a small fixed pixel amount (e.g. 6px x, 6px y per layer) so the back copies peek out from behind the front one — same layering idea as a physical stack of papers. Rendered as multiple stacked
elements sharing the same wave-bottom geometry, back-to-front.

Default size 160x120 (slightly larger than the single-document default to accommodate the offset peek). Text slot: only the front-most copy carries the text slot. Connection anchors: bbox fallback of the outermost (unioned) silhouette. Style surface: standard, but the back copies should probably use a slightly muted/tinted fill so the stack reads clearly (a small rendering nuance, not a schema field). Suggested name: new
, depends on Spec 9 landing first (or duplicates its own local wave geometry independently if sequencing requires).
A downward-pointing pentagon: a rectangle whose bottom edge is replaced by two angled edges meeting at a point below center — 5 vertices:

Default size 120x100. Text slot centered in the upper rectangular band. Connection anchors: 5-point polygon, true-outline tier (same treatment as the Pentagon spec above, just non-regular — this pentagon is NOT the same shape as the Basic tier's regular Pentagon, despite both being 5-sided; keep them as two separate specs/types). Style surface: standard. Suggested name: new
enum name for this is literally
— a naming curiosity flagged in the gap table; don't name our type
since that invites confusion with the Advanced tier's actual shield icon).
A 4-point trapezoid: wider bottom edge, narrower top edge, symmetric about the vertical center line. Points:
— a ~20% inset on the top edge only.
Default size 150x100. Text slot centered. Connection anchors: 4-point polygon, true-outline tier alongside diamond. Style surface: standard. Suggested name: new
A rectangle with a slanted top-left-to-top-right edge (top-left corner higher than top-right, or vice versa — FigJam's convention slants up from left to right). 4 points:
Default size 150x100. Text slot centered in the lower rectangular band. Connection anchors: 4-point polygon, true-outline tier. Style surface: standard. Suggested name: new

A 6-point regular hexagon, flat-top orientation (matching the flowchart "preparation" symbol convention), inscribed in the bounding box with independent x/y radius scaling to fill non-square aspect ratios.

Default size 150x100 (flowchart hexagons are typically wider than tall, unlike the Basic-tier's would-be square hexagon if Figma had one — it doesn't; this is Flowchart-only). Text slot centered. Connection anchors: 6-point polygon, true-outline tier. Style surface: standard. Suggested name: new

A rectangle with two additional straight rule lines drawn inside near the top edge and near the left edge (like a spreadsheet's frozen header row + column), each inset a fixed pixel amount (e.g. 12px) from its respective edge. Rendered as a rect plus two
elements — the same compositional strategy already used for
Default size 140x100. Text slot centered in the remaining lower-right two-thirds. Connection anchors: rectangular outline, so this can join the existing
-tier true-outline projection directly (no new polygon math needed — it's a plain rect with interior decoration, same as
). Style surface: standard, plus the two rule lines inherit the border color. Suggested name: new
— same "rect + inset rule marks" pattern, different mark placement).
A circle (true ellipse/circle outline, per the Ellipse spec above) with a centered 2-glyph overlay:
draws a plus/cross through the center;
draws an X through the center. Both overlays are simple 2-line SVG
pairs on top of the circle, sized to ~60% of the circle's diameter.

Default size 90x90 (small — these are junction glyphs, always roughly circular and compact, matching FigJam's own scale for this symbol pair). Text slot: none — these are pure junction glyphs, no label area, similar to
. Connection anchors: circular true-outline. Style surface: fill/stroke only, no strokeStyle variation needed (junction glyphs are always solid). Shipped types:
Where our model diverges from Figma's

props.title: Trim scale intentionally exceeds the measured baseline

text.insert: The selection toolbar is a 48px-tall rounded rect (radius 14) with 36px controls — deliberately larger than the measured 29px FigJam pill it supersedes. Flyouts open above the toolbar centered on their trigger button, and the color popover's swatches are 30px. The scaled-up values live in
(selectionToolbarHeightPx / selectionToolbarRadiusPx / selectionToolbarButtonHeightPx / colorPopoverSwatchPx) and render through
. This is a product decision, not a parity gap — any earlier measured-baseline figure for these trim surfaces is superseded by the deliberate scale-up.

props.title: Toolbar control sets deliberately simplified — no text-formatting tier

text.insert: One typeface and one text size everywhere: FigJam's bold/strikethrough/link/bullet-list/font/size/alignment controls are intentionally absent. Sticky bodies render live markdown today — H1–H3, bullets, bold, inline code (
). Per-type control sets: shape — color / text / shape-swap (
); section — color / rename / fit-children / border-style / lock (
). Connector toolbar buttons are icon-row controls; dash and arrowhead buttons reflect the selected connector's current value, and the color button reflects the current swatch.
One color pick per object, matching FigJam's model: every kind — shapes, stickies, sections, connectors — stores a single
swatch id from the closed 10-pick roster (FigJam's popover minus the custom wheel and minus black, D7/D12) and offers one pick per hue through the one shared
. The kind's palette role table decides rendering (

): ink is the picker swatch plus every stroke, fill is the shape/sticky/chip fill, and wash is the section tint. Every shape is bordered, white keeps gray strokes, and legacy `-soft` ids migrate to the bare hue id. Connectors deliberately deviate from source-color tinting: new connectors are always stamped gray, and a connector color pick updates only that connector. This replaced the earlier palette-token/tint split described elsewhere in this doc's history.
The design-intent palette sheet (per-hue ink/fill/wash swatches + hex table) lives at

Beyond the shape-by-shape gaps above, a few structural differences run through the whole model — these aren't fixable by adding one more shape type, they're differences in philosophy or scope between the two engines.

props.title: Closed enum vs. typed union — same philosophy, different vocabulary size

text.insert: is a closed string-literal union on

is exactly the same kind of closed union — neither engine has an open/extensible shape-registration mechanism at the schema level (Figma's plugin API confirms this: there's no way for a plugin to register a new
value, only the Widgets API sits outside the closed enum entirely, and that's a fully separate extension surface, not a shape). The two engines agree on the
of the problem; all 30 native rows (14 Basic + 16 Flowchart) are covered by our 30 geometric types, with
covering the glyph concepts on top.
— no rotation/angle field exists anywhere in the schema, renderer, or interaction code (confirmed by an exhaustive grep of the whole
hits are unrelated toolbar-icon and CSS-chevron animations in the trim layer, not object rendering). This is a deliberate, documented gap:

's own header comment states plainly that anchors are computed "directly against each object's axis-aligned bounds/outline" with "no rotation (this repo's schema has no per-object rotation field, unlike BlockSuite's
(and rotation generally across all node types) has no counterpart here at all — every shape spec above assumes an unrotated, axis-aligned object.
resolves to an anchor (one of 4 cardinal directions) plus an optional relative position, computed via a fixed anchor-offset/snap-radius cascade (
in connection-cascade.ts). Figma's connector endpoints instead expose a
NONE | AUTO | TOP | BOTTOM | LEFT | RIGHT | CENTER
, per the research report) — conceptually similar (both are ways of saying "attach here, and re-derive the exact point as the shape moves") but Figma's

magnet reads as more dynamic (letting Figma's own layout engine pick the best of several candidate points at render time) versus our fixed cascade which resolves to one concrete point per connection at edit time. Before W5, only 4 of our object shapes (
) got true non-bbox outline anchors — every other shape, including visually non-rectangular ones like

, fell back to its axis-aligned bounding-box outline for anchor projection. Since W5, that true-outline tier is much broader than the old four-shape note: ellipse, triangle, pentagon, octagon, star, off-page-connector, trapezoid, manual-input, hexagon, or-junction, and summing-junction also participate. Section interiors are intentionally not inside-tier connector targets, but their anchors and outlines still snap.

props.title: One arrowhead vs. twelve stroke caps

text.insert: We have exactly one arrowhead marker — a solid filled triangle, colored via SVG's
fill inheritance — with no per-connection choice. Figma's
, and others per the research report), settable independently per connector endpoint (start cap vs. end cap can differ). Our
connection style is also worth noting here as a stroke-level simplification: it uses the same routed path as
schema style values; the router itself is elbow/orthogonal by default, with explicit waypoints from bend editing as the override.

props.title: Corner radius: baked into the shape vs. a live Figma property

text.insert: Every corner radius in our renderer is a hardcoded constant, formerly in figjam-tokens.ts and now co-located with each def (
= 10, plain rounded-rect's 8px inlined directly in CanvasStage.tsx) or a CSS literal (pill/marker's 999px trick, diamond's clip-path has no radius concept at all).
field — there is no per-object override. Figma's
is instead a real (if read-only-via-some-APIs) per-node property that varies per instance, not a per-shape-type constant. Any future corner-radius customization here would need a new
field threaded through every shape branch in

props.covers: Gap analysis mapping FigJam's default shape picker (30 shapeType values + 3 connector presets + 26 Advanced component icons) against this engine's 17 object types, with full specs for every missing shape.

text.insert: Status: every spec below shipped in the W5 implementation wave —

through the icon-glyph family, all 20, are now real InteractiveCanvasObjectType values with working outlines, anchors, and text slots. These specs are no longer forward-looking design notes; they now double as the shape-API's worked examples, and the per-type reference lives in
